# UC-18.1.14 · Cisco ACI L3Out Prefix Monitoring (BGP/OSPF Border Routes, Withdrawal/Flap Detection at Fabric Boundary, l3extInstP External-EPG Health, bgpPeerEntry Session Tracking, Route-Map Filtering Audit)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Fault, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the outward-facing routes your building publishes toward carriers and remote offices—the paths packets take when traffic leaves campus—so upstream caretaker churn cannot masquerade as a silent outage for everyday apps.*

---

## Description

Each Cisco Application Centric Infrastructure **Layer-3 Outside** (**l3extOut**) hangs beneath a tenant **VRF** and stitches border-leaf forwarding toward WAN routers, MPLS provider-edge peers, Internet edges, cloud ExpressRoute-style hand-offs, or third-party racks. Beneath **l3extOut** sit **l3extLNodeP** logical-node profiles selecting leaves, **l3extLIfP** logical-interface profiles pinning routed interfaces, **l3extInstP** external endpoint groups expressing imported subnets plus **import-security** semantics, **bgpExtP** peer templates, **ospfExtP**/**ospfIfP** constructs, **eigrpExtP**, and **staticRoute** policies—together defining how externally sourced prefixes enter or leave the fabric.

Because policy rides **route-map** style constructs (**rtctrlSubjP**, **rtctrlAttrP**) attached to **l3extInstP**, quiet mistakes omit critical permits or strip **import-security**, starving dataplane flows without blowing classic interface optics alarms. Meanwhile **bgpPeerEntry** sessions expose neighbor **Established** posture plus prefix counters—yet upstream churn during MPLS reloads can mimic catastrophe unless correlated.

Splunk folds REST-fed **bgpPeerEntry**, **bgpRoute**, **ospfAdjEp**, **l3extOut** health, syslog **BGP-5-ADJCHANGE**, **BGP-3-NOTIFICATION**, **OSPF-5-ADJCHG**, faults **F0608**, **F1126**, and bespoke **l3out_prefix** aggregates into one pane so operators distinguish withdrawal storms (many prefixes vanishing together) from prefix flaps (one prefix oscillating).

## Value

Operational wins pair Splunk timelines with CMDB-backed expectation lists so advertised/learned prefix inventories remain auditable against design intent—highlighting drift after accidental **route-map** edits or mistaken **l3extSubnet** scope trims.

Splunk correlates **bgpPeerEntry** churn against **l3extOut** health dips and **l3ifInst** counters (**pktsDrop**, **pktsErr**) so teams escalate genuine egress threats during MPLS incidents instead of chasing harmless optics chatter.

Outcome themes include mandatory prefix-list hygiene checks tying **l3out_expected_prefixes.csv** hashes to polled advertisements, withdrawal-storm peaks stacked beside upstream-change markers, peer-flap timelines keyed by **peerAddr**, route-policy drift narratives referencing **rtctrl** audit lanes, and readiness drills tying fabric-border telemetry back to WAN carrier bridges.

## Implementation

Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (Splunkbase **4022**) on Heavy Forwarders plus Search Heads; layer HTTPS collectors hitting **aaaLogin.json** and **aaaRefresh.json** for **bgpPeerEntry**, **bgpRoute**, **ospfAdjEp**, **l3extOut**, **l3extInstP**, **l3extSubnet**, **bgpExtP**, **ospfExtP**, **staticRoute**, **vpcRsLifP**, **pcAggrIf**, **l3ifInst**, plus scripted **l3out_prefix** snapshots into **`index=cisco_aci`**. Encode **`l3out_expected_prefixes`** lookups from CMDB and route **`cisco:aci:syslog`** faults **F0608**, **F1126**. Schedule **`aci_uc1814_l3out_border`** every five minutes.

## Evidence

Saved search **`aci_uc1814_l3out_border`** on **`index=cisco_aci`** merging **`cisco:aci:bgp`**, **`cisco:aci:ospf`**, **`cisco:aci:l3out_prefix`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`** plus Dashboard Studio workbook **`ds_aci_l3out_border.json`** with **`tok_l3out`** and **`tok_peer`** drill tokens.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Cisco **APIC** clusters reachable over HTTPS **443/tcp** from Splunk collectors; dedicate automation identities isolated from GUI admins because overlapping `aaaRefresh.json` rotations collide across scripted collectors.
- Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) wherever **APIC** syslog terminates—preserve `sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog` narratives describing **BGP-5-ADJCHANGE**, **BGP-3-NOTIFICATION**, **OSPF-5-ADJCHG**, faults **F0608**, **F1126**.
- Splunk Heavy Forwarders host modular HTTPS collectors authenticated via `POST /api/aaaLogin.json` issuing `GET /api/class/bgpPeerEntry.json`, `GET /api/class/bgpRoute.json`, `GET /api/class/ospfAdjEp.json`, `GET /api/class/l3extOut.json`, `GET /api/class/l3extInstP.json`, `GET /api/class/l3extSubnet.json`, `GET /api/class/bgpExtP.json`, `GET /api/class/ospfExtP.json`, `GET /api/class/staticRoute.json`, `GET /api/class/l3ifInst.json`, `GET /api/class/vpcRsLifP.json`, `GET /api/class/pcAggrIf.json` at **60–120** second cadences—normalize outbound JSON into `sourcetype=cisco:aci:bgp`, `sourcetype=cisco:aci:ospf`, plus `sourcetype=cisco:aci:l3out_prefix` summarizers merging **prefix deltas**.
- Roles gain `srchIndexesAllowed` covering `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) plus scripted sourcetypes.
- Capacity planning treats dense `bgpRoute` pulls as **APIC** CPU sensitive—shard collectors across pods when **REST** latency climbs.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Package **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** (Splunkbase **4022**) then enable syslog forwarding so `cisco:aci:syslog` retains mnemonic faults referencing `bgpPeer`, `vrfdomain`, `l3extOut`.
(2) Implement modular HTTPS collectors emitting `sourcetype=cisco:aci:bgp` rows representing `bgpPeerEntry` fields (**peerAddr**, **peerAsn**, **state**, **holdTime**, **keepalive**, **prefixesReceived**, **prefixesSent**, **localAsn**) alongside Distinguished Names tying `l3extOut`, `bgpExtP`, tenant, `fvCtx`.
(3) Implement `sourcetype=cisco:aci:ospf` extracts from `ospfAdjEp` covering **adjacency states** aligned with `ospfExtP` and `ospfIfP` scopes for **border interfaces**.
(4) Author scripted `sourcetype=cisco:aci:l3out_prefix` polls merging `bgpRoute`, `ospfDb`, `rtctrlSubjP` permit tallies into learned versus advertised counters per `VRF`/** `l3extOut`, stamping withdrawal bursts versus flap counters using `withdrawn_burst` and `flap_same_pfx` math differentiated explicitly—**withdrawal storms** tally many distinct prefixes withdrawn concurrently while **prefix flaps** track identical prefixes toggling states.
(5) Land syslog faults **F0608** for BGP peers plus **F1126** (**vrfdomain**) alongside structured `l3out_expected_prefixes.csv` lookups authored nightly from CMDB declaring **aggregate hashes** per tenant `l3extOut`.
(6) Expected volume guidance—assume **~3–8 KB**/poll/**border peer** when `bgpRoute` snapshots remain summarized; escalate **Heavy Forwarder queues** before widening wildcard queries across `uni/tn-*` unintentionally.

### Step 2 — Splunk SPL search and alert configuration
Bookmark `aci_uc1814_l3out_border` referencing the canonical SPL bundled in this UC—schedule cron `*/5 * * * *` over `earliest=-24h@m`, throttle `tenant_lc` + `peer_ip` pairs for **30** minutes unless `wd_peak` doubles.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (
 sourcetype IN ("cisco:aci:bgp","cisco:aci:ospf","cisco:aci:l3out_prefix")
 OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(BGP-5-ADJCHANGE|BGP-3-NOTIFICATION|OSPF-5-ADJCHG|FULL\\s+to\\s+DOWN|F0608|F1126|vrfdomain)"))
 ) earliest=-24h@m
| eval lane=case(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:bgp","bgp",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:ospf","ospf",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:l3out_prefix","pfx",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","sf",1=1,"misc")
| eval tenant_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(tenant_name,tenant,fvTenant)))
| eval vrf_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(vrf_dn,vrf_name,fvCtx_dn,fvCtx)))
| eval l3out_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(l3extOut_dn,l3out_dn,l3out_name,l3extOut)))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peerAddr,peer_ip,nbr_ip))
| eval bgp_state_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(state,bgp_state,session_state)))
| eval ospf_adj_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(ospf_adj_state,adj_state,nbr_state)))
| eval prefixes_rcv=tonumber(coalesce(prefixesReceived,pfx_rcv,prefix_rcv_cnt))
| eval prefixes_adv=tonumber(coalesce(prefixesSent,pfx_adv,prefix_adv_cnt))
| eval withdrawn_burst=tonumber(coalesce(withdrawn_burst,wdr_burst_cnt,wdr_delta,pfx_withdraw_burst))
| eval flap_same_pfx=tonumber(coalesce(prefix_flap_same,same_prefix_flap_cnt))
| eval health_pct=tonumber(coalesce(l3out_health,l3ext_health_score,health_score))
| eval gap_pct=tonumber(coalesce(prefix_gap_pct,mismatch_pct))
| eval rtctrl_audit=trim(lower(coalesce(route_map_hit,rtctrl_match)))
| eval risk_bgp_down=if(lane=="bgp" AND NOT match(bgp_state_lc,"(?i)established"),1,0)
| eval risk_ospf_down=if(lane=="ospf" AND match(ospf_adj_lc,"(?i)(down|init|attempt|exstart|loading|2way)"),1,0)
| eval risk_health_drop=if(isnotnull(health_pct) AND health_pct<100,1,0)
| eval risk_fault=if(lane=="sf" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F0608|BGP.*down|peer.*down|vrfdomain)"),1,0)
| eval risk_wd=if(lane=="pfx" AND isnotnull(withdrawn_burst) AND withdrawn_burst>=250,1,0)
| eval risk_gap=if(lane=="pfx" AND isnotnull(gap_pct) AND gap_pct>=15,1,0)
| eval risk_flap=if(isnotnull(flap_same_pfx) AND flap_same_pfx>=10,1,0)
| where risk_bgp_down=1 OR risk_ospf_down=1 OR risk_health_drop=1 OR risk_fault=1 OR risk_wd=1 OR risk_gap=1 OR risk_flap=1
| bin _time span=5m as win5
| stats latest(lane) as ln latest(bgp_state_lc) as bgp_seen latest(ospf_adj_lc) as ospf_seen max(withdrawn_burst) as wd_peak latest(prefixes_rcv) as prcv latest(prefixes_adv) as padv latest(health_pct) as hp latest(gap_pct) as gp latest(rtctrl_audit) as rtc max(risk_fault) as mf max(risk_bgp_down) as mb max(risk_ospf_down) as mo max(risk_wd) as mw max(risk_gap) as mg max(risk_flap) as ml count as sig by win5 tenant_lc vrf_lc l3out_lc peer_ip
| lookup l3out_expected_prefixes vrf_lc l3out_lc OUTPUT maint_lane upstream_note
| eval suppress_hold=if(match(maint_lane,"(?i)(mpls_pe_maintenance_window|tcp_md5_rotation|dci_link_maintenance|import_security_review_window|bgp_aggregation_event|ldp_session_reset|ce_route_reflector_maintenance)"),1,0)
| where mf=1 OR mb=1 OR mo=1 OR hp<95 OR wd_peak>=400 OR mg=1 OR (suppress_hold=0 AND (mw=1 OR ml=1))
| sort - wd_peak - sig
| head 320
| table win5 tenant_lc vrf_lc l3out_lc peer_ip ln bgp_seen ospf_seen prcv padv wd_peak gp hp rtc sig maint_lane upstream_note
```

Correlation cues—the `risk_wd` lane captures withdrawal storms exceeding `250` deltas per bucket while `risk_gap` highlights `gap_pct` deltas versus `l3out_expected_prefixes` baselines; `risk_flap` isolates `flap_same_pfx` thresholds distinguishing oscillations from storms.

Macros recommended—wrap `threshold_withdraw_burst`, `threshold_gap_pct`, `threshold_flap` inside `macros.conf` so tuning stays declarative per tenant.

Alert routing sends `risk_fault` positives referencing **F0608** toward WAN/backbone queues while `risk_gap` rows involving `rtctrl_audit` traverse **policy governance guilds** reviewing `route-map` tuples without dragging unrelated spine-overlay drills (**UC-18.3.18** BGP-EVPN families).

### Step 3 — Validate
- **APIC** GUI **Tenants → Networking → Layer 3 Outside → pick tenant → choose object → Operational** compares `health_pct` plus `bgpPeerEntry` posture against Splunk `hp` fields inside five-minute buckets.
- Leaf CLI `vsh_lc -c "show bgp ipv4 unicast neighbor <peer>"` corroborates `bgp_seen` strings versus `cisco:aci:bgp` ingestion lag—differences wider than twice **poll cadence** imply collectors stalled.
- `moquery -c bgpPeerEntry` plus `moquery -c l3extInstP` sourced from **APIC** CLI replay Distinguished Names Splunk extracts—confirm `tenant_lc` tokens remain lowercase prior `stats` merges.
- Compare `l3ifInst` counters (**bytesRcv**, **bytesSnt**, **pktsDrop**) against **SNMP optics** dashboards—silent routing-policy faults sometimes precede drop spikes despite `Established` BGP markers.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard workbook `ds_aci_l3out_border.json` suggestion—Row **1** matrix `peer_ip` × `l3out_lc` colored by `bgp_seen`/** `ospf_seen` states; Row **2** timeline `wd_peak` stacked with `suppress_hold` overlays referencing `maint_lane` tokens; Row **3** ribbon `gap_pct` highlighting `risk_gap` breaches; Row **4** single-value `health_pct` tiles referencing `l3extOut` titles; Row **5** route-policy audit table contrasting `rtctrl_audit` snapshots versus archived `baseline_rtctrl.json` extracted nightly.

Incident bridgewalk anchors `vpcRsLifP` attachments plus `pcAggrIf` bundles—annotate floating **L3Out** designs tying VMware NSX-T hand-offs whenever `peer_ip` duplicates appear across redundant leaves.

Embed **Splunk Workflow Actions** linking `upstream_note` CMDB snippets referencing MPLS circuit IDs.

### Step 5 — Tuning and extensions
- Correlate with **UC-18.1.7** **APIC** `infraCluster` saturation—**REST** backlog lengthens `_indextime` skew misleading BGP timelines without implying WAN faults.
- Blend **UC-18.1.5** tenant `aaaModLR` audits whenever `rtctrl_audit` anomalies coincide with tenant-scope edits distinguishing deliberate publishes from drift.
- Feed upstream `mpls_pe_maintenance_window` markers via `CHG` ingestion suppressing benign `risk_wd` spikes whenever `suppress_hold` macros remain unset—maintain pairing between `maint_lane` literals enumerated inside `knownFalsePositives` and **Splunk lookups**.
- Tie WAN BGP telemetry from Cisco IOS XR or Arista EOS add-ons into `peer_ip` joins—never confuse intra-fabric **BGP-EVPN** overlays (**UC-18.3.18**) with **fabric-border** IPv4 sessions monitored here.
- Extend scripted `l3out_prefix` collectors with `predict` overlays forecasting ninety-percent `gap_pct` crossings once sixty-day histories stabilize.

Failure modes — `risk_health_drop` chatter without syslog corroboration often reflects stale `health_pct` extracts—bounce collectors after `aaaRefresh.json` HTTP **401** storms logged inside `splunkd.log`.

Sparse `peer_ip` fields imply `FIELDALIAS` gaps—promote camelCase synonyms `peerAddr`/** `peer_ip` explicitly inside `props.conf`.

Duplicate `stats` merges occur when collectors omit `collector_id` **dedupe keys**—stamp metadata before `stats` aggregation.

Lookup misses from `l3out_expected_prefixes` yield blank `maint_lane` rows—route positives through analyst review queues rather than auto-suppression until **CSV hygiene** improves.

Bold glossary anchors — **`bgpPeerEntry`** **`state`** tracks **Idle**, **Connect**, **Active**, **OpenSent**, **OpenConfirm**, **Established** transitions while **`l3extInstP`** **`l3extSubnet`** tuples expose **`aggregate`** scopes (**import-rtctrl**, **import-security**, **export-rtctrl**) pairing **`rtctrlSubjP`** **`rtctrlAttrP`** audits with **`route-map`** intent; **`vpcRsLifP`** **`floating L3Out`** attachments ride **`pcAggrIf`** bundles yet still honor **`vsh_lc`** leaf verification drills. Tie **`risk_gap`** positives with **carrier BGP** dashboards plus **east-west isolation** reviews whenever overlays mimic faults.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci (
 sourcetype IN ("cisco:aci:bgp","cisco:aci:ospf","cisco:aci:l3out_prefix")
 OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(BGP-5-ADJCHANGE|BGP-3-NOTIFICATION|OSPF-5-ADJCHG|FULL\\s+to\\s+DOWN|F0608|F1126|vrfdomain)"))
 ) earliest=-24h@m
| eval lane=case(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:bgp","bgp",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:ospf","ospf",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:l3out_prefix","pfx",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","sf",1=1,"misc")
| eval tenant_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(tenant_name,tenant,fvTenant)))
| eval vrf_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(vrf_dn,vrf_name,fvCtx_dn,fvCtx)))
| eval l3out_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(l3extOut_dn,l3out_dn,l3out_name,l3extOut)))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peerAddr,peer_ip,nbr_ip))
| eval bgp_state_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(state,bgp_state,session_state)))
| eval ospf_adj_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(ospf_adj_state,adj_state,nbr_state)))
| eval prefixes_rcv=tonumber(coalesce(prefixesReceived,pfx_rcv,prefix_rcv_cnt))
| eval prefixes_adv=tonumber(coalesce(prefixesSent,pfx_adv,prefix_adv_cnt))
| eval withdrawn_burst=tonumber(coalesce(withdrawn_burst,wdr_burst_cnt,wdr_delta,pfx_withdraw_burst))
| eval flap_same_pfx=tonumber(coalesce(prefix_flap_same,same_prefix_flap_cnt))
| eval health_pct=tonumber(coalesce(l3out_health,l3ext_health_score,health_score))
| eval gap_pct=tonumber(coalesce(prefix_gap_pct,mismatch_pct))
| eval rtctrl_audit=trim(lower(coalesce(route_map_hit,rtctrl_match)))
| eval risk_bgp_down=if(lane=="bgp" AND NOT match(bgp_state_lc,"(?i)established"),1,0)
| eval risk_ospf_down=if(lane=="ospf" AND match(ospf_adj_lc,"(?i)(down|init|attempt|exstart|loading|2way)"),1,0)
| eval risk_health_drop=if(isnotnull(health_pct) AND health_pct<100,1,0)
| eval risk_fault=if(lane=="sf" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F0608|BGP.*down|peer.*down|vrfdomain)"),1,0)
| eval risk_wd=if(lane=="pfx" AND isnotnull(withdrawn_burst) AND withdrawn_burst>=250,1,0)
| eval risk_gap=if(lane=="pfx" AND isnotnull(gap_pct) AND gap_pct>=15,1,0)
| eval risk_flap=if(isnotnull(flap_same_pfx) AND flap_same_pfx>=10,1,0)
| where risk_bgp_down=1 OR risk_ospf_down=1 OR risk_health_drop=1 OR risk_fault=1 OR risk_wd=1 OR risk_gap=1 OR risk_flap=1
| bin _time span=5m as win5
| stats latest(lane) as ln latest(bgp_state_lc) as bgp_seen latest(ospf_adj_lc) as ospf_seen max(withdrawn_burst) as wd_peak latest(prefixes_rcv) as prcv latest(prefixes_adv) as padv latest(health_pct) as hp latest(gap_pct) as gp latest(rtctrl_audit) as rtc max(risk_fault) as mf max(risk_bgp_down) as mb max(risk_ospf_down) as mo max(risk_wd) as mw max(risk_gap) as mg max(risk_flap) as ml count as sig by win5 tenant_lc vrf_lc l3out_lc peer_ip
| lookup l3out_expected_prefixes vrf_lc l3out_lc OUTPUT maint_lane upstream_note
| eval suppress_hold=if(match(maint_lane,"(?i)(mpls_pe_maintenance_window|tcp_md5_rotation|dci_link_maintenance|import_security_review_window|bgp_aggregation_event|ldp_session_reset|ce_route_reflector_maintenance)"),1,0)
| where mf=1 OR mb=1 OR mo=1 OR hp<95 OR wd_peak>=400 OR mg=1 OR (suppress_hold=0 AND (mw=1 OR ml=1))
| sort - wd_peak - sig
| head 320
| table win5 tenant_lc vrf_lc l3out_lc peer_ip ln bgp_seen ospf_seen prcv padv wd_peak gp hp rtc sig maint_lane upstream_note
```

## Visualization

(1) Matrix — **peer_ip** × **l3out_lc** with **Established** badges versus fault hues. (2) Timechart — withdrawn-burst peaks versus prefix-gap percentages. (3) Single-value — border **l3extOut** **health_pct** tiles under **100**. (4) Table — **rtctrl_audit** deltas versus baseline snapshots. (5) Histogram — flap_same_pfx density distinguishing flap storms.

## Known False Positives

**mpls_pe_maintenance_window** — Scheduled MPLS provider-edge reboots intentionally recycle **bgpPeerEntry** states through **Active** toward **Established** while upstream optics heal; Splunk velocity resembles outages yet dataplane forwarding often resumes behind aggregates once convergence completes—suppress rows tied to CAB references naming PE/router scopes rather than paging pure BGP choreography noise.

**bgp_aggregation_event** — Internet carriers occasionally consolidate many specifics behind wider aggregates during tariff reshuffles; Splunk learns fewer discrete prefixes while traceroutes remain stable via summarized coverage—require numeric correlation proving dataplane impact before blaming **l3extInstP** edits alone.

**tcp_md5_rotation** — Operators rotate BGP TCP MD5 secrets during credential hygiene; neighbors bounce briefly yet resume **Established** quickly—stamp automation transcripts beside Splunk timelines before Sev-2 escalation.

**dci_link_maintenance_window** — Dark-fibre crews patch interconnect strands forcing redundant border-leaf pairs through graceful BGP resets—pair Splunk bursts with facility tickets referencing strand IDs rather than silent fabric regressions.

**import_security_review_window** — Security engineers temporarily reshape **import-security** tuples while auditing external routes—Splunk may flag subnet removals matching maintenance narratives yet intentional pauses must never masquerade as silent dataplane sabotage absent corroborating ticket lanes.

**ldp_session_reset** — MPLS label-distribution churn can ripple label stacks without collapsing adjacency narratives entirely—BGP next hops may jitter briefly though **Established** strings persist—tie positives to upstream MPLS trouble queues referencing **ldp_session_reset** stamps before rewriting **route-map** intent.

**ce_route_reflector_maintenance** — Customer-edge clusters migrating route-reflector anchoring shift best-path advertisements—Splunk observes withdrawal-like churn reflecting topology churn rather than wholesale prefix loss—cross-check BGP additive-path telemetry plus WAN diagnostics.

- [Cisco APIC Layer 3 Networking Configuration Guide (Release 6.0(x))](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/dcn/aci/apic/6x/configuration/b-cisco-apic-layer-3-networking-configuration-guide-60x/b-cisco-apic-layer-3-networking-configuration-guide-60x.html)
- [Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI (Splunkbase 4022)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)
- [Splunk Lantern — operational analytics guidance](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
- [Cisco APIC REST API Configuration Guide — Managed Object Queries (Release 6.0(x))](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/dcn/aci/apic/6x/rest-cfg/cisco-apic-rest-api-configuration-guide-60x.html)
